12.anchor: what has been entrusted to me.
12.note: Or what I have entrusted to him

10.anchor: has deserted me and gone to Thessalonica. Crescens has gone to Galatia,

21.anchor: Do your best to come before winter. Eubulus sends greetings to you, as do Pudens and Linus and Claudia and all the brothers.
21.note: Or brothers and sisters. In New Testament usage, depending on the context, the plural Greek word

15.anchor: Do your best to present yourself to God as one approved,
15.note: That is, one approved after being tested

21.anchor: if anyone cleanses himself from what is dishonorable,

24.note: For the contextual rendering of the Greek word doulos

14.anchor: continue in what you have learned and have firmly believed, knowing from whom

17.note: That is, a messenger of God (the phrase echoes a common Old Testament expression)
